cards.back: Importing libraries in Python is done using the "import" keyword.
NumPy (Numerical Python) is a library for working with arrays and mathematical operations.
pandas is a library for data manipulation and analysis in Python.
Matplotlib is a library for creating static, animated, and interactive visualizations in Python.
Pandas DataFrames are two-dimensional labeled data structures with columns of potentially different types.
Data visualization is the process of creating graphical representations of data to communicate insights.
Data preprocessing involves cleaning, transforming, and feature engineering data to prepare it for modeling.
Scikit-learn is a library for machine learning in Python that provides a wide range of algorithms for classification, regression, clustering, and more.
XGBoost is an optimized distributed gradient boosting library for Python that provides high-performance, scalable, and accurate predictive models.
Decision trees are a type of supervised learning algorithm that uses a tree-like model to classify data or make predictions.
Random forests are an ensemble learning method that combines multiple decision trees to improve the accuracy and robustness of predictions.
Cross-validation is a technique used to evaluate the performance of machine learning models by splitting data into training and testing sets.
Overfitting occurs when a model is too complex and fits the training data too closely, resulting in poor performance on unseen data.
Handling missing values involves identifying, imputing, or removing missing data to ensure that the dataset is complete and accurate.
